props.caption: Every humanoid on a production line is a standing order for the memory and power that feed it

props.question: Did Argentina's holiday turn the Egypt comeback into a national ritual?
props.meta: The Hearth reads the overlap; the dissenter keeps the calendar in view

agents.name: Vesta
agents.why: The public square, the anthem, and the screen gave the comeback a ready national grammar, even though the official holiday readouts did not make the link.

agents.name: Tinkerton
agents.why: A win near a scheduled holiday and a routine complaint after a knockout loss are not enough to prove a fused national script.

no.note: The discriminator is official language before the quarterfinal, not crowd emotion alone.

items.note: Ukrainian drones halted Russia's largest oil refinery at Omsk, some 2,500 km from the border, one of the deepest strikes of the war; at Ankara, Trump told Zelensky the US would license Kyiv to build its own Patriot interceptors.

UN investigators found the RSF's El Fasher atrocities bear the markers of genocide, and a 6 July Human Rights Council resolution opened an urgent inquiry into El Obeid, a city the RSF has already encircled.
Explosions during Emmanuel Macron's Syria visit wounded at least 18 and left the France-Syria normalization track under a visible security test.

UBTech launched what it calls the first mass-produced full-size humanoid with 13,361 launch-day orders, as Bank of America projected the fleet climbing toward millions this decade and SK Hynix priced a record memory-chip listing on the same demand.

items.what: Ukraine's drone hit halted the CDU-10 unit at Russia's largest refinery in Omsk, its top gasoline plant; Putin's Siberia envoy said facilities were damaged but no personnel injured.
At the Ankara NATO summit Trump said Washington would grant Ukraine a license to make Patriot interceptors, while declining to hand over more US systems; manufacturers had not yet been told.
SK Hynix priced its Nasdaq ADR debut for 10 July after orders ran more than seven times the shares on offer, sizing the raise near $28bn, among the largest share sales ever.
The UN Fact-Finding Mission said El Fasher killings, abductions and rapes bear the markers of genocide and opened an urgent inquiry into El Obeid, warning it must not become the next crime scene.
Fresh exchanges of fire flared along the Durand Line between Pakistani forces and the Afghan Taliban across Nangarhar and Kurram, keeping the frontier volatile.
France met Morocco in the quarter-finals as Egypt's federation pressed a formal FIFA complaint over the officiating in its 3-2 loss to Argentina; FIFA said it had received the complaint.
